'''Ancestor worship''' ({{nihongo|sosen sūhai|祖先崇拝}}) is a religious practice centered on the veneration of deceased family members, based on the belief that ancestors continue to influence the living. In Japan, ancestor worship is a core element of both {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} and {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|ja|日本の仏教|lt=Japanese Buddhism}}, commonly expressed through household {{ill|kamidana|ja|神棚}} and {{ill|butsudan|ja|仏壇}}, and through observances such as {{ill|Obon|ja|お盆}} and {{ill|higan|ja|彼岸}}.

{{stub}}
[[Category:Pages without wikidata]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
[[Category:Translated pages]]
